metadata.purpose: Template for AI to report newly discovered Qualia types during extraction
metadata.usage: AI populates this structure in _NEW_QUALIA sheet when encountering properties not in em_qualia_types.json

proposed_id.description: Snake_case identifier for the new qualia (e.g., 'scaffolding_holes')

proposed_category.description: Which main category this qualia belongs to

description.description: Clear definition of what this qualia represents
description.example: Regular holes in masonry indicating original scaffolding positions during construction

source_quote.description: Verbatim quote from source document where this property was mentioned
source_quote.example: "i fori pontai sono disposti su tre file orizzontali" [p. 23]

source_document.description: Document reference where this qualia was encountered

suggested_values.description: For controlled vocabularies, suggested allowed values

rationale.description: Why this qualia is distinct from existing ones and worth adding
rationale.example: No existing qualia captures construction trace evidence; closest is construction_technique but that's about method, not physical traces

similar_existing.description: Most similar existing qualia (if any) and why it's insufficient
similar_existing.example: construction_technique - covers methods but not physical evidence of construction process

occurrence_count.description: How many times this property type was encountered in the current extraction

xlsx_sheet_format.notes: One row per proposed new qualia. This sheet is for human review and vocabulary expansion.

examples.description: Holes in masonry walls indicating original scaffolding pole positions
examples.source_quote: "nella muratura sono visibili i fori pontai disposti su tre ordini" [p. 45]
examples.rationale: Common construction evidence in Roman masonry; needed for accurate reconstruction of building process
examples.similar_existing: construction_technique covers method but not physical traces

examples.description: Thickness of waterproof cocciopesto/opus signinum layer
examples.source_quote: "lo strato di cocciopesto ha uno spessore variabile tra 2 e 4 cm" [p. 12]
examples.rationale: Specific measurement for waterproofing layers; general 'thickness' is for the whole US, not specific layers
examples.similar_existing: thickness - but that's for the whole unit, not internal layers

examples.description: Original provenance or building of reused (spolia) materials
examples.source_quote: "i blocchi calcarei provengono probabilmente dalla demolizione del tempio repubblicano" [p. 78]
examples.rationale: Important for understanding building chronology and material economy; goes beyond simple material_type
examples.similar_existing: material_type identifies what, not where from

when_to_create_entry: Property mentioned in source documents that doesn't match any existing qualia
Property with distinct semantic meaning from similar existing qualia
Property that appears multiple times across different units, suggesting it's a pattern not an anomaly
Property with clear definition and potential controlled vocabulary
when_not_to_create_entry: Property is just a variant name for existing qualia (use existing qualia instead)
Property is a one-off observation better captured in free-text description
Property is too specific to a single site/context to be generalizable
Property can be expressed as a combination of existing qualia
quality_criteria: Has clear data type and (if applicable) controlled vocabulary
Includes source evidence with verbatim quote
